module_mood_contract: Agents 101 supplementary — take-home catalogue. No single-module mood contract; serves the full arc. Practitioner-peer register, 'this is how you do it' confidence. Intro callout at body top sets expectation that move-by-move detail lands as each module stabilises.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase scores: 8, 8, 9, 8, 7, 9. Close: 9. No phase below 7. Phase 5 (Recipes 5–8, five consecutive shape-only stubs) scores 7 — facilitator-premium threshold, not REVISE-triggering. The body callout at intro ('move-by-move write-ups land as each module's content stabilises, recipe shape first, full detail as it earns the ink') earns the 7: the student is not confused, merely reading a known-partial document. Previous REVISE triggers (Phase 1 mood 6 from exposed maintainer process notes; Phase 5 mood 6 from same) are resolved: process notes moved below <!-- maintainer -->, callout added to body top.
Standalone supplementary, not an arc module. No inter-module mood arc to preserve.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Core teaching moments: (1) 'you are the one evaluator you can't fool on your own content' — Recipe 1 why, lands cleanly for Agents 101 SVP HR persona. (2) Worked examples (R2 PM agent, R3 research system, R4 email triage): each ends on a named surprise that is the pedagogical payload. All three land at 8–9 in trace. (3) Index of agent shapes: immediately business-recognisable for this persona (QBR brief, monthly-close commentary). For Recipes 5–8 (shape-only), the teaching moment is bounded by the intro callout: 'recipe shape first, full detail as it earns the ink.' The test-and-why sections partially land the move's logic without the step-by-step. Not a full teaching-moment delivery for R5–R8, but not a structural failure — appropriately framed as progressive.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — Agents 101 module Big Ideas honored per recipe.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Recipe 1 honors M1 Big Idea ('With the right guardrails, you create output that's genuinely yours — not generic'). Recipe 2 honors M2 ('A system remembers, grows, and compounds around the one thing you need to make progress on'). Recipe 3 honors M3 fan-in multi-agent shape. Recipe 4 honors M4 security discipline ('certainty about agent safety is a fantasy; the discipline is running the check'). Recipes 5–8 honor M5–M8 respectively at shape level. No recipe inverts or front-runs its module's Big Idea.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No conversation cues, pause beats, 'discuss with...', 'share with your neighbor', or other cohort-management sentences in body. Supplementary reference document; trainer presence is not assumed. Body is entirely about the work (recipes, worked examples, index archetypes).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary reading; no student-copy prompt blocks present. Rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement (meta-frame as closer, not opener).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Supplementary reading, not a lecture. No placement judgment required.

Recipe 4: 'certainty about agent safety is a fantasy' — epistemic, not punitive. Recipe 5: 'a judge picked from a scoreboard you ran is one you'd defend' — confidence framing, not error-exposure. No recipe frames the student as having done something wrong.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Every recipe title leads with a positive discipline name: 'A piece of output that is genuinely yours', 'A compounding system', 'A scoped agent your organisation can trust', 'A judge you've earned by benchmarking', 'A self-improving generator', 'Sharing a slice', 'A flywheel that builds the next agent'. No failure-mode title framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic Agents 101 themes recur across recipes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Compounding theme: R2 ('a chat can't compound; a folder of markdown can'). Personal-evaluator theme: R1 ('you are the one evaluator you can't fool on your own content'). Empirical-discipline theme: R5 ('quality-method selection in agent work is empirical, not authoritative'). Human-moves-up-a-level theme: R6 ('the human moves up a level — you stop reviewing every output and start designing the loop'). Absorption-not-access theme: R7 ('the bottleneck isn't can they reach it, but can they fit it into their existing work'). Multiple Agents 101 throughlines present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Standalone supplementary. No module hand-off to evaluate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop leak (cookbook sits on strategy/curriculum boundary).
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'After Agents 101, you own them' (intro) is informational (identifies recipe origin) not promotional (no pricing, features, or sales vocabulary). Body text stays in practitioner-peer register throughout. Strategy-doc vocabulary does not intrude into recipe bodies.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: TODO (non-blocking): 'After Agents 101, you own them' could imply exclusion for readers who haven't taken Agents 101. Consider 'These first three are the Agents 101 training's spine — and they stand alone' to preserve the informational note without the gating implication.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Phase 5 mood 7 — facilitator premium, non-blocking TODO.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[5].mood_score: 7. Five consecutive shape-only stubs (R5–R8) produce fatigue after the worked-example peaks of R2–R4. The 7 is appropriate given the intro callout has set expectation correctly. Would lift to 8 when one worked example lands per recipe (per build order in maintainer block: Pass 2 for each module as it reaches Pass 3). Non-blocking; no fix required at this pass.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: When each module reaches Pass 3, promote the module exercise body into a standalone worked example in the matching recipe. Priority by student appetite: R5 (judge benchmarking — high drama) and R8 (flywheel — high aspiration) first.

stealing_the_mood: Phase 5 (Recipes 5–8): five consecutive shape-only stubs without worked examples — mood 7/10 (facilitator-premium, non-blocking). Intro callout has set the right expectation; this is a known-partial state, not a structural failure. Would lift to 8 on first worked example per recipe.
